name: when-setting-network-security-use-network-security-setup
description: Configure Claude Code sandbox network isolation with trusted domains, custom access policies, and environment variables for secure network communication.
capabilities: Audit network requirements and external dependencies
Design network security policies with whitelist approach
Implement firewall rules and DNS filtering
Configure environment variables for network settings
Test access controls and validate policies
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: All 49 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains only documentation with example templates in markdown code blocks. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are misidentified GraphViz DOT syntax and ASCII art diagrams. 'External command' detections are example bash scripts shown as documentation, never executed. This is a legitimate network security configuration guide.
valueStatement: Claude Code sandbox needs secure network configuration to prevent unauthorized access while allowing trusted domains. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for configuring firewall rules, trusted domains, and access policies.
limitations: Does not modify actual system firewall rules
Requires manual execution of configuration scripts
Cannot verify network policies without actual deployment

useCases.title: Configure firewall rules
useCases.description: Set up iptables firewall policies to control inbound and outbound network traffic for Claude Code sandbox.

useCases.title: Define trusted domains
useCases.description: Create whitelist of trusted domains like GitHub, npmjs, and PyPI for secure external access.

useCases.title: Document security policies
useCases.description: Generate comprehensive documentation for network security configuration and deployment procedures.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up network security for Claude Code sandbox with trusted domains and firewall rules.

promptTemplates.title: Trusted Domains
promptTemplates.prompt: Define trusted domains for Claude Code sandbox including Anthropic API, GitHub, npmjs, and PyPI.

promptTemplates.title: Firewall Rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure iptables firewall to allow only HTTPS, HTTP, and SSH outbound traffic.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement complete network security setup: firewall rules, DNS filtering, rate limiting, and environment variables for Claude Code sandbox.

bestPractices: Use whitelist approach - deny all traffic by default and explicitly allow trusted domains
Implement rate limiting to prevent abuse and protect external API endpoints
Regularly audit and update trusted domains as project requirements evolve
antiPatterns: Using blacklist approach instead of whitelist for network security
Allowing all outbound traffic without restrictions
Not testing firewall rules before deployment

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code on all platforms.

faq.question: What ports should I allow?
faq.answer: Allow outbound 80, 443, 22 and inbound 3000, 5000, 8000, 8080 for development.

faq.question: How do I integrate with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Save policy JSON files to version control and apply them during deployment pipeline.

faq.question: Is my data sent externally?
faq.answer: This skill only configures policies. No data is sent externally by the skill itself.

faq.question: What if network tests fail?
faq.answer: Check firewall rules allow the port, verify domain spelling, and ensure rate limits are not exceeded.

faq.question: How is this different from cloud security groups?
faq.answer: This skill configures sandbox-level isolation. Use with cloud security groups for defense in depth.
